string.templatelib/Template/interpolatio.name: interpolations
string.templatelib/Template/interpolatio.description: A tuple of the interpolations in the template. >>> cheese = 'Camembert' >>> template = t'Ah! We do have {cheese}.' >>> template.interpolations (Interpolation('Camembert', 'cheese', None, ''),) The interpolations tuple may be empty and always contains one fewer values than the strings tuple: >>> t'Red Leicester'.interpolations ()

string.templatelib/Interpolation.name: Interpolation
string.templatelib/Interpolation.description: The Interpolation type represents an expression inside a template string. It is immutable, meaning that attributes of an interpolation cannot be reassigned. Interpolations support pattern matching, allowing you to match against their attributes with the match statement: >>> from string.templatelib import Interpolation >>> interpolation = t'{1. + 2.:.2f}'.interpolations[0] >>> interpolation Interpolation(3.0, '1. + 2.', None, '.2f') >>> match interpolation: ... case Interpolation(value, expres...
